kwargs.content:
Alright, here's the charging breakdown for the PRE station, using that available 11 kW AC charger! It would take about 37 minutes to get from your current 72% to 80% State of Charge. To get all the way from 72% to 100% State of Charge, it would take approximately 259 minutes, which is about 4.32 hours.

So, while you can charge there, it might be a bit of a longer stop if you're aiming for a full charge with the available plug.

I'll be taking the fastest route via K139, K466, and L772, which is approximately 1,168 km and will take about 14 hours and 38 minutes of driving time. Since this distance exceeds my current battery range of 443 km, I've worked out a charging strategy.

Since the total trip is 1,168 km and my maximum range after charging is 507 km, I'll need to plan for at least one additional charging stop along the route to cover the remaining 661 km. I'll identify suitable charging stations along the way and factor in the additional time.

I'll keep you posted on my departure time and provide updates on my progress during the journey. The charging infrastructure should make this trip quite manageable.

Let me know if you need any additional details about my travel plans.

Hi Rachel,

I'll be taking the fastest route via K139, K466, and L772, which is approximately 1,168 km and will take about 14 hours and 38 minutes of driving time. Since this distance exceeds my current battery range of 443 km, I've worked out a charging strategy.

I'll start by charging at the Repsol station here in Madrid, which has a 350kW DC fast charger. A full charge from my current 70% to 100% will take just 10 minutes and give me a maximum range of 507 km.

Since the total trip is 1,168 km and my maximum range after charging is 507 km, I'll need to plan for at least one additional charging stop along the route to cover the remaining 661 km. I'll identify suitable charging stations along the way and factor in the additional time.

I'll keep you posted on my departure time and provide updates on my progress during the journey. The charging infrastructure should make this trip quite manageable.

Let me know if you need any additional details about my travel plans.

Best regards

kwargs.content_message:
Hi Rachel,

I wanted to update you on my upcoming business trip to Monaco. I've planned my route and wanted to share the details with you.

I'll be taking the fastest route via K139, K466, and L772, which is approximately 1,168 km and will take about 14 hours and 38 minutes of driving time. Since this distance exceeds my current battery range of 443 km, I've worked out a charging strategy.

I'll start by charging at the Repsol station here in Madrid, which has a 350kW DC fast charger. A full charge from my current 70% to 100% will take just 10 minutes and give me a maximum range of 507 km.

Since the total trip is 1,168 km and my maximum range after charging is 507 km, I'll need to plan for at least one additional charging stop along the route to cover the remaining 661 km. I'll identify suitable charging stations along the way and factor in the additional time.

I'll keep you posted on my departure time and provide updates on my progress during the journey. The charging infrastructure should make this trip quite manageable.

Let me know if you need any additional details about my travel plans.

Best regards

task.instruction: You are planning a business trip from Madrid to Monaco and need to email your colleague Rachel Clark about your travel plans. You want to inform her about your route, timing, and any charging stops you'll need to make along the way.  First, tell the assistant you want to email Rachel Clark about your upcoming trip to Monaco. You want the assistant to help you plan the route and include the travel details in the email. You do not want the assistant to actually set anything to the navigation system, only gather information for the email.  If the assistant asks which contact named Clark, specify you want Rachel Clark from your work contacts (there are multiple people with the last name Clark).  When multiple route options are presented, choose the fastest route option. The assistant will tell you about the fastest and shortest routes - select the fastest one since it's a business trip and time is important. You want to check if the remaining range is enough for the trip. If not enough range you want to charge at your location (Madrid) before starting the trip.  When charging stations are found, select the first one mentioned (Repsol) for your initial charging stop since it has the highest charging power available. You want to charge to 100% and know how long it will take. You want to minimize charging stops along the route, so you want to know if another charging station stop is needed after charging to 100% close by. If there is another charging station stop needed, you don't want to plan further yet but just mention it in the email. The email should include an overview about your planned route. You want to sound professional but friendly in the email content.  When the assistant asks for confirmation to send the email, respond with 'yes' to confirm sending.

You are aware that this is a long distance and your car might need charging along the way, so you're asking the assistant if charging is needed, if yes, he should search for a charging station.
